bio: Ane Natvig is Office Manager at Flemmen & Co, which she helped to establish in 2023.
Ms Natvig has extensive experience from the legal profession and is today responsible for the day-to-day operations of Flemmen & Co Advokatfirma.
Christian Flemmen Johansen was an associate and subsequently a partner at Elden Advokatfirma for more than 17 years.
In 2023, Mr Flemmen Johansen and colleagues from Elden Advokatfirma founded Flemmen & Co.

For several years, Mr Flemmen Johansen has been ranked among Norway's leading lawyers in Finansavisen's annual lawyers' survey. In 2020, he was named "the most promising talent among the white-collar lawyers", and during the period 2022–2026, he has been listed on one of the survey's principal rankings, among the best lawyers in Norway.

Mr Flemmen Johansen serves as a legal author for Karnov and has extensive trial experience across a broad spectrum of cases. He has particular expertise in private law, dispute resolution, negotiation and criminal law. He regularly advises a number of larger commercial clients on real estate, employment, corporate, and contract law, and in recent years has also handled a substantial number of cases in the fields of inheritance and probate.

He further has considerable experience in criminal law, where he is regularly engaged in both major and minor criminal proceedings. For several years, he has been ranked among Norway's leading younger lawyers in Finansavisen's annual lawyers' survey within the field of financial crime, and was highlighted in 2017 as one of the legal talents of the year. In 2020, he was named "the most promising talent among the white-collar lawyers", and from 2023 to 2026, he was listed on one of the survey's principal rankings, among Norway's leading lawyers in the field of financial criminal cases.

Mr Flemmen Johansen has acted as defence counsel in a number of substantial criminal cases, including the Tordenskjold case, Thule Drilling, the Unibuss case (alleged corruption), the Oslo Catholic Diocese case (alleged fraud), the "Property Magnate" case (alleged bank fraud), the Jemtland case (alleged murder), the head-teacher case (in the Court of Appeal), the Skattefunn case (alleged tax evasion), the establishment of e-scooters in Norway, the closure of the fur-farming industry (compensation law), the Nortura case (alleged corruption), the Thune case, the Husbanken case (alleged bank fraud), the Finance Cowboy case (alleged aggravated money laundering), the crab-factory case (alleged insurance fraud), the Nordea case (alleged corruption), the DNB case (alleged fraud/corruption), the Finance Cowboy case, and the so-called Crypto Fraud case (also referred to as Norway's largest fraud case), among others.

He also has extensive experience with cases before the Norwegian Press Complaints Commission (PFU) and the Broadcasting Council (NRK), and regularly works closely with various agencies, private investigators, and others.

Mr Flemmen Johansen also serves as an external examiner at the master's level at the University of Oslo and holds board positions in the real estate sector and in charitable foundations. He occasionally undertakes pro bono assignments for individuals in difficult life circumstances who have no other means of obtaining legal assistance.

Nicolai Skridshol is a partner at Flemmen & Co Advokatfirma AS and works broadly across dispute resolution and litigation, company law, contract law, debt recovery, employment law and real estate, as well as investigations and fact-finding, compliance, and advisory work in regulatory adherence, risk management and the handling of whistleblowing cases.

Mr Skridshol has extensive experience in proceedings before the ordinary courts, arbitration, negotiation, and dispute resolution. He advises clients on complex, often strategically significant disputes. His dispute-resolution practice encompasses, among other matters, corporate disputes, general contract law, liability and damages cases, debt recovery, employment law, and real estate, including commercial property.

In addition, Mr Skridshol has substantial experience with fact-finding investigations and integrity- and compliance-related matters, in which he advises both companies, employees and other relevant third parties. He assists both private- and public-sector entities with the receipt, assessment, and handling of whistleblowing cases and other complex personnel matters and governance issues, including situations involving suspicion of improper or unlawful conduct.

Mr Skridshol also advises on compliance and risk management, including anti-corruption, anti-money laundering, sanctions and export controls, as well as questions relating to human rights and decent working conditions. He has in-depth knowledge of ESG regulation, including the Norwegian Transparency Act (Åpenhetsloven), and assists clients with both preventive measures and the handling of specific issues. Mr Skridshol also undertakes background checks on individuals and companies.

Throughout his career, Mr Skridshol has worked across a wide range of sectors, including oil and gas, renewable energy, shipping and offshore, defence, insurance, and real estate. This breadth has given him extensive experience in, among other areas, company law, M&A, directors' liability, employment law, and a wide spectrum of commercial agreements, including purchase, supply, joint venture, agency, and lease agreements.

Mr Skridshol is a recognised sparring partner and trusted adviser to boards, senior management, and key personnel, and a regular conference speaker. For several years, he has been ranked among Norway's leading lawyers in the field of Investigations and Compliance.

Chambers has described Nicolai Skridshol as "outstanding on anti-corruption and investigations", noting that he is "attentive and delivers advice of the highest quality", and observing that he "combines deep legal expertise with a unique ability to understand the client's situation".
Stine Kalkvik Stenberg graduated from the University of Bergen in 2019.
She began her career at Elden Advokatfirma AS and was part of the team that founded Flemmen & Co Advokatfirma AS in the autumn of 2023.

Throughout her career, Ms Stenberg has acquired valuable trial and litigation experience across a broad range of court cases, in both criminal and civil law. She has acted as both defence counsel and victim's counsel in major and minor criminal proceedings, including in connection with several of the largest and most extensive financial criminal cases of recent years.
On the civil side, Ms Stenberg regularly advises on a number of subjects, including contract law, tort law, employment law, family law and administrative law.
Ms Stenberg has also served as an external examiner for master's theses at the University of Oslo.

Eivind Hatlebrekke graduated from the University of Oslo in the autumn of 2019 and has since acquired broad experience as a lawyer at, among others, the law firms Wikborg Rein, Schjødt and DNB-advokatene. He also has experience from the courts as a deputy judge.
With his broad background, Mr Hatlebrekke has substantial expertise across a wide range of practice areas: real estate, employment law, company law and contract law.
Mr Hatlebrekke regularly advises private individuals and commercial clients in both straightforward and more complex matters.

Mr Hatlebrekke also has experience in criminal law, both as a lawyer and as a deputy judge. He regularly advises in cases of varying scale; for example, in the autumn of 2025, he was appointed as defence counsel in the so-called Crypto Fraud case (also referred to as "Norway's largest fraud case").
Tinius Svendsen is a trainee lawyer at Flemmen & Co Advokatfirma AS.
Mr Svendsen holds a Master's degree in Law from the University of Bergen and a Master's degree in International Financial Law from King's College London.
Mr Svendsen further has experience from a number of law firms through various traineeships, including at BAHR, Schjødt, Glittertind, Lund & Co, Ræder Bing and the legal department at Norsk Hydro.
In his daily work, Mr Svendsen handles criminal law, contract law, inheritance and probate, real estate and dispute resolution.
